topics.title: Dynamic Brain Connectivity Across States and Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain dynamics and functional connectivity vary across brain states, consciousness, and the adult lifespan. It emphasizes network neuroscience approaches for characterizing individualized brain patterns, including fingerprints, state transitions, and structure-function relationships.

topics.title: Alzheimer’s Disease and Neurodegenerative Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer’s disease and related neurodegenerative conditions, with emphasis on cognitive decline, dementia risk, and disease progression across the Alzheimer continuum. It highlights biomarkers such as tau, amyloid burden, and brain atrophy in aging and preclinical disease.

topics.title: Neural Correlates of Memory and Attention
topics.description: This cluster centers on how working memory, attention, and recall are represented in the brain, with emphasis on neural correlates, encoding, and hippocampal involvement. It also highlights variability across individuals and functional/dynamic interactions measured with fMRI in human visual and memory systems.

topics.title: Lesion-Related Network Changes Across Neurologic Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on how focal brain lesions and neurological diseases alter brain networks, cognition, and recovery. It spans stroke, traumatic injury, multiple sclerosis, and neurodegenerative disorders, with emphasis on functional and structural connectivity changes.

topics.title: Depression and Connectivity Alterations
topics.description: This cluster centers on depressive and related psychiatric disorders, especially major depressive disorder, adolescent depression, and treatment resistant depression. It emphasizes brain connectivity and associated clinical factors such as childhood trauma, postpartum depression, and suicidal ideation.

keywords: 75μm isotropic human brain ex vivo dataset
topics.title: High-Resolution MRI Atlases and Quantitative Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on high-resolution MRI of the human and mouse brain, including quantitative mapping, atlas construction, and comparisons across field strengths and modalities. It also links MRI findings with histology and reproducibility-focused acquisition protocols.

topics.title: Noninvasive and Deep Brain Network Modulation
topics.description: Studies in this cluster examine how stimulation methods such as tms, dbs, and temporal interference stimulation alter brain networks and behavior. The abstracts emphasize causal evidence, functional connectivity, and clinical applications across stroke, parkinson disease, and treatment resistant depression.

topics.title: Neural bases of language and reading
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the neural correlates of language, speech perception, and reading across typical and atypical development. It includes studies of dyslexia, aphasia, and multilingual speakers using fMRI and fNIR to probe language processing and comprehension.

topics.title: Deep Learning for Brain MRI and Alzheimer’s
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning approaches applied to brain MRI, especially for automated segmentation and Alzheimer’s-related classification and progression analysis. It emphasizes convolutional and 3D models, including transfer learning and foundation models, for neuroimaging tasks.

topics.title: Cortical Folding and Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on human cortical folding, surface organization, and atlas-based characterization of brain structure across development. It also includes connectivity and segmentation approaches used to quantify and compare cortical anatomy.

topics.title: Neural bases of emotion regulation and anxiety
topics.description: This cluster centers on how emotion regulation, especially cognitive reappraisal, relates to amygdala and prefrontal mechanisms during emotion processing. It also includes anxiety, valence, uncertainty, reward, and developmental or self-related factors that shape neural and behavioral responses.

topics.title: Transdiagnostic Psychosis and Schizophrenia Research
topics.description: This cluster centers on psychosis-related psychiatric disorders, especially schizophrenia, early psychosis, and spectrum conditions, with attention to heterogeneity, negative symptoms, and clinical prediction. It also spans related disorders such as bipolar disorder and depression in a transdiagnostic framework.

topics.title: Brain Development and Outcomes Across Ages
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain development and neuroimaging findings in vulnerable populations, especially preterm birth, congenital heart disease, and neonatal hypoxic-ischemic injury. It links white matter and cortical measures to cognitive outcomes across infancy, adolescence, youth, and adulthood.

topics.title: Neurodevelopmental Brain Connectivity Across Childhood
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain development from infancy through adolescence, with emphasis on structural and functional connectivity changes and their relationships to behavior. It also includes work on atypical developmental trajectories and neurodevelopmental assessment.

topics.title: Epilepsy Dynamics and Localization
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying epileptogenic and seizure onset regions in epilepsy, especially temporal lobe and pediatric cases. It also emphasizes brain connectivity and dynamic network characterization using electrophysiology and related analyses.

topics.title: Neuroimaging Data Harmonization and Infrastructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, collaborative neuroimaging infrastructure for managing, harmonizing, and processing imaging data across consortia. It emphasizes BIDS-based workflows, containerized tools, and platform support for scalable, reproducible analysis.

topics.title: Aging, Metabolism, and Brain Vascular Function
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging and healthy aging relate to human brain metabolism, cerebrovascular physiology, and network function. It also connects autonomic and cardiovascular factors to brain insulin action and default mode network changes across the adult lifespan.

topics.title: Adolescent Brain, Risk, and Depression
topics.description: Studies focus on how genetic and environmental risk factors relate to brain structure, neurodevelopment, and psychopathology across childhood and adolescence. A major emphasis is the association between brain anatomy and depressive symptoms, resilience, and broader mental health outcomes.

keywords: autism sensory subtypes predict task evoked insula connectivity
abnormal functional hierarchy development during childhood in autism
topics.title: Autism Subgroups and Childhood Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on autism spectrum disorder in children and autistic youth, emphasizing symptom variation, subgroups, and developmental changes in brain function. It highlights heterogeneous clinical patterns and altered functional organization during childhood.

topics.title: Substance Use Craving and Neurocircuitry
topics.description: This cluster centers on addiction-related substances, craving, reward, and abstinence across alcohol, cannabis, opioid, and methamphetamine use disorders. It also includes adolescent brain development and substance exposure as related factors in risk and neurocircuitry.

topics.title: High-Resolution fMRI and Visual Network Mapping
topics.description: The cluster centers on high-resolution and layer-specific fMRI methods for mapping visual and resting-state brain function, often in combination with EEG or other concurrent measurements. It also includes studies of visual processing, cortical blindness, and network-level responses under different task and resting conditions.

keywords: deep neural network representational alignment predict behavioral performance
topics.title: Neural Encoding and Model Alignment
topics.description: This cluster centers on relating brain responses and human brain function to deep neural network and large language model representations, especially through brain encoding and representational alignment. It also includes work on cross-subject prediction, behavioral performance, and human AI team interactions.

keywords: functional connectivity pathways support fear avoidance processes
topics.title: Brain Mechanisms of Chronic Pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on chronic pain conditions, including trigeminal neuralgia and widespread pain, and how they relate to pain perception, central sensitization, and analgesia. It also highlights brain-based markers and structural or connectivity differences that may underlie pain processing and fear-avoidance pathways.

keywords: intrinsic striatal functional brain connectivity signature of compulsivity
topics.title: OCD Networks and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder, especially frontostriatal and corticostriatal network alterations linked to compulsivity and related anxiety. It also includes psychotherapy and CBT-related predictors of treatment response.

keywords: the dieter 's dilemma attentional focus on health
topics.title: Neural control of eating and obesity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how brain networks, attention, and executive functioning relate to food choices, distracted eating, and appetite regulation in obesity and related disorders. It also includes clinical contexts such as type 2 diabetes, bariatric surgery, binge eating disorder, and food insecurity.

keywords: music and emotion in functional connectivity
topics.title: Neural mechanisms of music engagement and reward
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music listening, training, and participation shape reward, emotion, memory, and sensorimotor brain systems. It also includes social and performance aspects of music, alongside neural pathways and decoding approaches linked to musical expertise and engagement.

keywords: choroid plexus volume mediates the link between insomnia severity and memory impairment
nonlinear eeg complexity reveal sleep relate neural signatures
topics.title: Sleep Signatures, Aging, and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG-based characterization of sleep and related neural signatures, including sleep stage structure, insomnia, obstructive sleep apnea, and aging-related brain changes. It also highlights links between sleep, functional connectivity, and memory impairment, with some work aimed at prediction using wearable or multimodal measures.

keywords: brain sensitivity across the menstrual cycle an eeg study
topics.title: Hormonal Influences on Brain Sex Differences
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how hormones and reproductive states shape brain structure, connectivity, and sensitivity across the lifespan. It also includes studies of sex differences and cross-species comparisons of hormone-related neural effects.

keywords: modular human ipsc derived neuronal network
topics.title: Neural Network Circuit Mechanisms and Models
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuronal network organization, circuit mechanisms, and computational modeling of brain activity. It also includes biologically inspired network systems such as reservoir computing, iPSC-derived neuronal networks, and associative memory models.

topics.title: Inner Speech and Prefrontal Causality
topics.description: Studies examining the causal role of the prefrontal operculum in inner speech, often using lesion symptom mapping evidence. The cluster focuses on how brain damage informs understanding of language-related internal speech processes.

keywords: brain age gap reduction following exercise mirrors clinical improvements
topics.title: Exercise and Brain Aging in Schizophrenia
topics.description: This cluster centers on the relationship between exercise-related brain age changes and clinical improvement in the schizophrenia spectrum. It suggests a link between physical activity, neural aging, and symptom outcomes.
